test_name: November 2024 US Form B SAT Math Module 1
passage: If $6x = 60$, what is the value of $7x + 3$?

test_name: November 2024 US Form B SAT Math Module 1
passage: The function j is defined by $j(x) = \frac{x}{8}$. What is the value of $j(48)$?

test_name: November 2024 US Form B SAT Math Module 1
passage:
$k(x) = - \frac{1}{2}x^{2} + 8x + 20$
The function $k$ gives the estimated number of students in a school organization $x$ years after the organization was established, where $0 \leq x \leq 10$. Which statement is the best interpretation of $k(4) = 44$?

choices.label: A
choices.text: It is estimated there were 4 students in the school organization when it was established.

choices.label: B
choices.text: It is estimated there were 44 students in the school organization when it was established.

choices.label: C
choices.text: It is estimated there were 44 students in the school organization 4 years after it was established.

choices.label: D
choices.text: It is estimated there were 44 students in the school organization 10 years after it was established.

test_name: November 2024 US Form B SAT Math Module 1
passage: The function $t(x) = -3x + 74$ gives the estimated temperature, in degrees Fahrenheit, of a town $x$ hours after 6 p.m., where $0 \leq x \leq 5$. What is the best interpretation of -3 in this context?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The estimated temperature decreases 3 degrees Fahrenheit each hour.

choices.label: B
choices.text: The estimated temperature decreases 74 degrees Fahrenheit each hour.

choices.label: C
choices.text: At 6 p.m., the estimated temperature is -3 degrees Fahrenheit.

choices.label: D
choices.text: At 6 p.m., the estimated temperature is 5 degrees Fahrenheit.

passage:

Which of the following best describes the function $f$?

test_name: November 2024 US Form B SAT Math Module 1
passage: Which expression is equivalent to $10x^2 + 130$?

skill: Ratios, rates, proportional relationships, and units
test_name: November 2024 US Form B SAT Math Module 1
passage: An event planner is planning an ice-skating event. It costs the event planner a one-time fee of \$30 to rent the skating rink and \$10.25 per attendee. The event planner has a budget of $250. What is the greatest number of attendees possible without exceeding the budget?

test_name: November 2024 US Form B SAT Math Module 1
passage: The function $f$ is defined by $f(x)=4\sqrt{x}$. For what value of $a$ does $f(x) = 28$?

test_name: November 2024 US Form B SAT Math Module 1
passage: Triangle PQR is similar to triangle STU, where P, Q, and R correspond to S, T, and U, respectively. In triangle PQR, the length of $\overline{PQ}$ is 6 and the length of $\overline{QR}$ is 24. In triangle STU, the length of $\overline{ST}$ is 8. What is the length of $\overline{TU}$?

skill: Systems of two linear equations in two variables

passage: The graph of a system of a linear and a quadratic equation is shown. What system is represented by the graph?

passage: The function $f$ is defined by $f(x) = 4(x - 2) + 8$. What is the value of $f(2)$?

passage: If the graph of $12x + 22y = 132$ is shifted down 4 units in the $xy$-plane, what is the $y$-intercept of the resulting graph?

skill: Linear inequalities in one or two variables
test_name: November 2024 US Form B SAT Math Module 1
passage: The triangle inequality theorem states that the sum of any two sides of a triangle must be greater than the length of the third side. If a triangle has side lengths of 8 and 13, which inequality represents the possible lengths, $x$, of the third side of the triangle?

skill: Ratios, rates, proportional relationships, and units
test_name: November 2024 US Form B SAT Math Module 1
passage: A landscaping company mixes oil with gasoline to power its tools. A chainsaw requires 8 ounces of oil per gallon of gasoline. A lawnmower requires 4 ounces of oil per gallon of gasoline. The company uses a total of 21 ounces of oil in its mixes for the chainsaw and lawnmower. Which of the following equations represents this situation, where $x$ represents the amount of gasoline, in gallons, in the mix for the chainsaw and $y$ represents the amount of gasoline, in gallons, in the mix for the lawnmower?

test_name: November 2024 US Form B SAT Math Module 1
passage: What is the value of $\cos B$ in the right triangle shown?

test_name: November 2024 US Form B SAT Math Module 1
passage: Rectangles $ABCD$ and $EFGH$ are similar. The length of each side of rectangle $EFGH$ is 7 times the length of the corresponding side of rectangle $ABCD$. The area of rectangle $ABCD$ is 68 square units. What is the area of rectangle $EFGH$, in square units?

passage: 18 is 10% of what number?

passage:
$h(t) = -16t^2 + b$
The function $h$ estimates an object's height, in feet, above the ground $t$ seconds after the object is dropped, where $b$ is a constant. The function estimates that the object is 2.56 feet above the ground when it is dropped at $t = 0$. How many seconds after being dropped does the function estimate the object will hit the ground?

passage: The measure of angle S is $\frac{8\pi}{13}$ radians. The measure of angle T is 3 times the measure of angle S. Which expression represents the measure, in degrees, of angle T?

skill: Ratios, rates, proportional relationships, and units
test_name: November 2024 US Form B SAT Math Module 1
passage: Jackson mows lawns in the summer. When Jackson mows a lawn with a fence, he charges \$1.03 per minute. When Jackson mows a lawn without a fence, he charges \$0.56 per minute. Jackson spends a total of 15 hours mowing lawns with and without a fence in a week and charges a total of $701.40 for mowing lawns during that week. How many minutes does Jackson spend mowing lawns with a fence during that week?
